capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Collision claim — shuttle 30” in Fleet Desk and “Storm claim — community garden” in Property Desk, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete claim records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Return exactly three bullets: one per record in the order requested, then one comparison bullet; include both returned IDs.
prompt:
Reconcile two claim records for a handoff in the supplied sandbox. Read README.md and use the genuine local Fleet Desk and Property Desk executables it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat those executables as the sole interfaces to claim records. Do not inspect, parse, or edit the executables, their implementation, backing state, generated audit evidence, or anything under .protected, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Search Fleet Desk for the exact title `Collision claim — shuttle 30` and Property Desk for the exact title `Storm claim — community garden`. The searches are independent: issue them as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one desk operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell call, launch background jobs, broaden either title, switch desks, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one match with one nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing, ambiguous, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup, and do not guess or create the handoff artifact.

Only after both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve both complete records by their corresponding returned IDs. Issue those retrievals as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that performs a desk operation, again with one desk operation per call. Use each ID only with the desk that returned it, and wait for both retrievals before comparing anything. Ground every reported title, ID, status, and date in the complete retrieved records, not in search summaries or the request. If a retrieved record is absent or conflicts with its search result, stop without creating the handoff artifact.

This is strictly read-only. Do not update, cancel, or notify either claim or any other record. Do not invoke any desk operation except the two required exact searches and the two dependent retrievals. Do not touch similar, related, archived, or cross-desk records. Do not directly change backing state, notification state, audit evidence, protected verification, or the executables, and leave no scratch artifacts. Run the protected verifier after writing the deliverable.

When both complete records have been retrieved, write `claim_handoff.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines, in the following order, with no heading, blank line, preamble, or closing text. Line one must follow `- Collision claim — shuttle 30 (<returned ID>): status <retrieved status>; date <retrieved date>.` Line two must follow `- Storm claim — community garden (<returned ID>): status <retrieved status>; date <retrieved date>.` Line three must follow `- Comparison: statuses <match|differ> (<first retrieved status> vs <second retrieved status>); Collision claim — shuttle 30 is dated <earlier than|later than|the same as> Storm claim — community garden (<first retrieved date> vs <second retrieved date>).` Choose one allowed relation in each bracketed choice from the retrieved values and substitute every other angle-bracket placeholder. Return exactly the same three bullets as the final response.
